tree.text: The Chinese Writing System: Connecting the Past and the Present
tree.cn: 中国文字系统：连接过去与现在
tree.notes: 阐述汉字作为书写系统在中国古代文明延续中的作用，从甲骨文演变到现代书法及其国际影响力。
tree.statement: 本文指出，汉字书写系统是中国古代文明得以延续的主要因素之一。
tree.explanation: 文章第一段提到，中国古代文明得以持续，其中一个主要因素就是汉字书写系统。

children.text: Paragraph 1: Civilization & Writing System (文明与文字系统)
children.cn: 第一段：古老文明与作为关键因素的汉字书写系统
children.notes: 说明中国古代文明即使历经兴衰依然延续，书写系统在其中起到了关键的纽带作用。
children.statement: despite 后面可以接句子作状语。
children.explanation: despite 是介词，后面只能接名词、代词或动名词（名词短语），不能直接接句子。

children.text: China is widely known for its ancient civilisation which has continued all the way through into modern times, despite the many ups and downs in its history.
children.cn: 中国以其历史悠久的古代文明而闻名于世，尽管其历史上经历了许多起伏，但这一文明一直延续到现代。
children.notes: which引导定语从句修饰 civilisation；all the way through 意为“一直”；despite 是介词，意为“尽管”。
children.statement: ups and downs 意为“兴衰，起伏”。
children.explanation: ups and downs 指人生的浮沉、国家或历史的兴衰、事物的起落。
children.keywords: widely known, civilisation, despite, ups and downs
children.highlight: is widely known for, which has continued, despite

children.text: There are many reasons why this has been possible, but one of the main factors has been the Chinese writing system.
children.cn: 这之所以成为可能，有很多原因，但主要因素之一是汉字书写体系。
children.notes: why引导定语从句修饰 reasons；one of the main factors 作主语，谓语动词用单数 has been。
children.statement: why 引导的定语从句在句中作原因状语。
children.explanation: why 在此处作关系副词，修饰 reasons，引导限制性定语从句，在从句中担任原因状语。
children.highlight: reasons why, one of the main factors

children.text: Paragraph 2: Picture-based Origins (图画起源与甲骨文)
children.cn: 第二段：汉字起源于以图画为基础的符号，可追溯至甲骨文
children.notes: 叙述早期汉字的起源，提到龙骨（longgu）和符号雕刻。
children.statement: 本段指明，最初的书面汉语是一种以声音为基础的语言。
children.explanation: 文章指出，起初书面汉语是一种以图画为基础的语言（picture-based language）。

children.text: At the beginning, written Chinese was a picture-based language.
children.cn: 起初，书面汉语是一种以图画为基础的语言。
children.notes: written Chinese 书面汉语；picture-based 以图画为基础的。
children.statement: picture-based 属于复合形容词，修饰 language。
children.explanation: 名词+过去分词构成复合形容词，表示“以……为基础的”。
children.highlight: At the beginning, was a

children.text: It dates back several thousand years to the use of longgu—animal bones and shells on which symbols were carved by ancient Chinese people.
children.cn: 它可以追溯到几千年前使用龙骨的时期——动物骨头和甲壳，上面刻有符号。
children.notes: date back to 追溯到；on which 是“介词+关系代词”结构引导的定语从句，修饰 bones and shells。
children.statement: on which 也可以替换成 where。
children.explanation: 先行词是 bones and shells (具体实物)，并不是地点，因此不能用关系副词 where 替换，只能用 on which。
children.keywords: dates back, longgu, bones, shells, carved
children.highlight: dates back several thousand, on which, were carved by

children.text: Some of the ancient symbols can still be seen in today's hanzi.
children.cn: 一些古老的符号在今天的汉字中仍然可见。
children.notes: can still be seen 是含情态动词的被动语态；hanzi 指汉字。
children.statement: seen 是 see 的过去分词形式。
children.explanation: see - saw - seen，seen 是过去分词，常用于被动语态或完成时态中。
children.highlight: Some of, can still be seen in

children.text: Paragraph 3: Well-developed System (商朝的成熟体系)
children.cn: 第三段：商朝文字的发展及地理割裂引发的方言变体
children.notes: 叙述商朝时期书写体系走向成熟，但割裂的社会导致了方言和字符的变体。
children.statement: 在商朝之后，各地区的发展导致了方言和汉字字形的统一。
children.explanation: 相反，当时因为地理上的割裂（divided geographically），导致了多种方言和汉字字形的变体（varieties of dialects and characters）。

children.text: By the Shang Dynasty, these symbols had become a well-developed writing system.
children.cn: 到了商代，这些符号已发展成为一个成熟的书写体系。
children.notes: By + 过去时间点，主句用过去完成时 had become；well-developed 成熟的，发达的。
children.statement: By the Shang Dynasty 作为时间状语，表示“到商朝为止”。
children.explanation: By 后面接过去的时间点时，句中谓语动词通常要用过去完成时，表示在此时间点前已完成的动作。
children.highlight: By the, had become a

children.text: Over the years, the system developed into different forms, as it was a time when people were divided geographically, leading to many varieties of dialects and characters.
children.cn: 多年来，该体系发展成不同的形式，因为当时人们在地理上是分裂的，导致了许多方言和文字的变体。
children.notes: when 引导定语从句修饰 time；leading to 是现在分词短语作结果状语。
children.statement: leading to 作为伴随状语，表示状态的延伸。
children.explanation: leading to 在此句中是现在分词短语作结果状语，表示“导致了……”这一自然发生的结果。
children.keywords: developed, geographically, leading to, dialects, characters
children.highlight: developed into, as it was a time when, leading to

children.text: Paragraph 4: Qin Unification (秦朝的大一统)
children.cn: 第四段：秦始皇统一文字及其对凝聚国家的重要意义
children.notes: 讲述秦朝统一七国，推行书同文，使中华民族无论何处何种方言均可通过书面交流。
children.statement: 统一的书写系统对凝聚中国人民和文化起到了极大的推动作用。
children.explanation: 第四段提到：That writing system was of great importance in uniting the Chinese people and culture.

children.text: This, however, changed under Emperor Qinshihuang of the Qin Dynasty.
children.cn: 然而，这一切在秦朝秦始皇统治下发生了改变。
children.notes: however 是副词作插入语，前后用逗号隔开；under 意为“在……统治下”。
children.statement: however 在这里可以用 but 替换，位置完全一样。
children.explanation: but 是连词，用于连接句子，通常放在句首，不能像副词 however 那样作为插入语放在主语后面。
children.highlight: This, however, changed under

children.text: Emperor Qinshihuang united the seven major states into one unified country where the Chinese writing system began to develop in one direction.
children.cn: 秦始皇将七个主要国家统一为一个统一的国家，在此基础上汉字书写系统开始向一个方向发展。
children.notes: united ... into ... 统一成……；where 引导定语从句修饰 country，并在从句中作地点状语。
children.statement: where 引导的从句是修饰 states 的定语从句。
children.explanation: where 引导的定语从句先行词是 country，而不是 states。
children.keywords: united, major states, unified country, develop
children.highlight: united the seven, into one, where the, began to

children.text: That writing system was of great importance in uniting the Chinese people and culture.
children.cn: 那一书写体系在团结中国人民和文化方面具有重要意义。
children.notes: be of great importance 意为“非常重要”，相当于 be very important；in uniting 意为“在团结……中”。
children.statement: of great importance 等同于 very important。
children.explanation: “be of + 抽象名词”结构在语法上相当于“be + 该名词对应的形容词”。
children.highlight: was of great importance in

children.text: Even today, no matter where Chinese people live or what dialect they speak, they can all still communicate in writing.
children.cn: 即使在今天，无论中国人生活在哪里，说什么方言，他们仍然可以通过书面进行交流。
children.notes: no matter where 引导让步状语从句；communicate in writing 通过书写交流。
children.statement: no matter where 可以用 wherever 替换。
children.explanation: “no matter + 疑问词”引导让步状语从句时，均可与“疑问词-ever”形式互换。
children.highlight: Even today, no matter where, communicate in

children.text: Paragraph 5: Connection to the Past & Art (古今连接与书法艺术)
children.cn: 第五段：汉字连接古今，且发展成为独特的中国书法艺术
children.notes: 汉字连接了过去与现在，使得现代人能够直接阅读古人的经典名著，并演化为书法艺术。
children.statement: 汉字可以让现代人直接阅读古代中国人写下的经典作品。
children.explanation: 文中提到：People in modern times can read the classic works which were written by Chinese in ancient times.

children.text: Written Chinese has also become an important means by which China's present is connected with its past.
children.cn: 汉字也已成为连接中国现在与过去的重要手段。
children.notes: means 单复数同形，这里前面有 an 限制，作单数；by which 引导定语从句，修饰 means。
children.statement: means 在这里是 mean 的第三人称单数形式。
children.explanation: means 在这里是名词，意为“方式，手段，方法”，单复数同形，并不是动词 mean 的单三形式。
children.keywords: become, important means, present, connected, past
children.highlight: has also become an, means by which, is connected with

children.text: People in modern times can read the classic works which were written by Chinese in ancient times.
children.cn: 现代人能够阅读中国古代人写的经典著作。
children.notes: which 引导定语从句，修饰 works；classic works 经典著作；ancient times 古代。
children.statement: which 引导的定语从句中，谓语动词 were written 采用了被动语态。
children.explanation: 经典作品(works)是被写出来的，所以使用过去时被动语态 were written。
children.keywords: modern times, read, classic works, ancient
children.highlight: can read the, which were written by, in

children.text: The high regard for the Chinese writing system can be seen in the development of Chinese characters as an art form, known as Chinese calligraphy, which has become an important part of Chinese culture.
children.cn: 对汉字书写体系的高度重视表现在汉字作为一种艺术形式的发展上，即人们所熟知的中国书法，它已经成为中国文化的重要组成部分。
children.notes: known as 过去分词短语作后置定语；which 引导非限制性定语从句，修饰 Chinese calligraphy。
children.statement: known as 在句中充当后置定语，修饰 Chinese characters 作为艺术形式的这一表现形式。
children.explanation: known as Chinese calligraphy 作后置定语修饰前面的 art form。
children.keywords: high regard, art form, known as, calligraphy, culture
children.highlight: The high regard for, can be seen in, known as, which has become

children.text: Paragraph 6: Modern Global Role (当代全球角色与欣赏)
children.cn: 第六段：当代汉字依然是中国文化的核心，并走向全球
children.notes: 汉字仍然是中国文化的重要载体，随着中国走向世界，越来越多的外国学生开始欣赏中国文化。
children.statement: 越来越多的国际学生正在通过学习汉语来欣赏中国的文化和历史。
children.explanation: 最后一段指出：an increasing number of international students are beginning to appreciate China's culture and history through this amazing language.

children.text: Today, the Chinese writing system is still an important part of Chinese culture.
children.cn: 今天，汉字书写体系仍然是中国文化的重要组成部分。
children.notes: still 依然；important part of ... 的重要部分。
children.statement: writing system 是动名词短语充当名词修饰 system。
children.explanation: writing 是动名词，修饰 system 表示该系统的用途和性质。
children.keywords: Today, writing system, still, important part
children.highlight: is still an important part of

children.text: As China plays a greater role in global affairs, an increasing number of international students are beginning to appreciate China's culture and history through this amazing language.
children.cn: 随着中国在全球事务中发挥更大作用，越来越多的留学生开始通过这门神奇的语言欣赏中国的文化和历史。
children.notes: As 引导时间/让步/伴随状语从句；an increasing number of 越来越多的，后接可数名词复数，谓语动词用复数 are；appreciate 欣赏，感激。
children.statement: an increasing number of 后面接复数名词，但谓语动词要用单数形式。
children.explanation: an increasing number of（越来越多的）修饰复数名词作主语时，谓语动词用复数。若是 the number of...（……的数量），谓语动词才用单数。
children.keywords: plays a role, global affairs, increasing number, appreciate, amazing
children.highlight: As China plays a, role in, an increasing number of, are beginning to, through this

tree.cn: 英语学习（在线论坛讨论）
tree.notes: 记录了王乐、刘文、贾欣、李瑞关于英语学习中听力、口语礼貌、词汇记忆等方面的问题及相互提出的建议。
tree.statement: 本论坛讨论围绕学生在学习英语时遇到的最大困难展开。
tree.explanation: 王乐的第一个发帖就问：what are your biggest problems with learning English?

children.text: Wang Le: What are your biggest problems with learning English?
children.cn: 王乐：大家学习英语最大的问题是什么？
children.notes: 王乐发帖引导大家讨论英语学习中的主要困难。
children.statement: 王乐在帖子中分享了他自己学习英语的诀窍。
children.explanation: 王乐并没有分享自己的诀窍，而是向大家提问，询问大家在英语学习中遇到的最大困难。

children.text: Wang Le: Hey, everybody, what are your biggest problems with learning English?
children.cn: 王乐：嘿，大家学习英语最大的问题是什么？
children.notes: problems with doing sth 在某方面的问题/困难。
children.statement: problems with 后面不能接名词，只能接动名词。
children.explanation: problems with 后面既可以接名词也可以接代词或动名词。例如 problems with grammar 也是正确的。
children.highlight: what are your, problems with

children.text: Liu Wen: Listening Problems with Native Speakers
children.cn: 刘文：与英语母语者交流时的听力困难
children.notes: 刘文阐述了自小学起学英语，但听母语者说话时很难听懂大意的问题。
children.statement: 刘文觉得自己在听力方面没有任何问题。
children.explanation: 刘文明确提到，他现在在听力上面临很多困难，看视频时只能听懂几个词。

children.text: Liu Wen: Hi! I've been studying English since primary school.
children.cn: 刘文：嗨！我自小学起就一直在学英语。
children.notes: have been studying 现在完成进行时，表示动作从过去开始一直持续到现在，并有可能继续下去；since + 时间点。
children.statement: since primary school 表明动作发生在过去且已彻底结束。
children.explanation: since primary school 与现在完成进行时连用，强调学习英语这一动作从过去一直延续至今，并没有结束。
children.highlight: I've been studying, since

children.text: I used to get high marks in English, but now I'm having a lot of trouble with my listening.
children.cn: 我以前英语成绩很好，但现在我在听力上面临很多困难。
children.notes: used to do 过去常常（现在已不这样）；have trouble with sth 做某事有困难。
children.statement: used to get 表示过去常常得高分，而现在并不是了。
children.explanation: used to 表示过去常常，暗示与现在的状况形成对比。
children.keywords: used to, high marks, having trouble, listening
children.highlight: I used to, but now I'm having, trouble with

children.text: When I listen to native English speakers talking in a video, I can catch only a few words.
children.cn: 当我听视频中英语母语者说话时，我只能听懂几个词。
children.notes: listen to 听；speakers talking 现在分词短语作宾语补足语，表示动作正在发生；only a few 仅仅几个。
children.statement: a few 表达否定概念，表示几乎没有。
children.explanation: a few 表达肯定概念，表示“一些，几个”；表示否定概念“几乎没有”的是 few（不带冠词 a）。
children.keywords: listen to, native speakers, catch, few words
children.highlight: When I listen to, talking in a, catch only a

children.text: I can never quite get the main idea.
children.cn: 我怎么也听不太懂大意。
children.notes: never quite 绝不完全，怎么也不；get 理解，明白；main idea 大意，主旨。
children.statement: quite 在句中修饰动词 get。
children.explanation: 副词 quite 在句中作状语，修饰动词 get，表示程度。
children.highlight: I can never quite, the main

children.text: Any advice?
children.cn: 有什么建议吗？
children.notes: 省略句，完整形式为 Do you have any advice?；advice 是不可数名词。
children.statement: advice 是可数名词，可以用复数形式 advices。
children.explanation: advice 是不可数名词，不能加s，表示多条建议可用 pieces of advice。
children.highlight: Any

children.text: Jia Xin: Suggestions for Listening & Politeness in English
children.cn: 贾欣：听力建议与英语中的礼貌表达
children.notes: 贾欣建议通过听英语广播节目以及录音对比来克服听力困难，同时提出在英语里礼貌表达（如使用Would you mind...）的困惑。
children.statement: 贾欣认为在英语里直接说 'Open the window!' 听起来非常得体和礼貌。
children.explanation: 贾欣提到，虽然直接说 'Open the window!' 要容易得多，但在英语中这听起来非常糟糕（不礼貌）。

children.text: Jia Xin: Listening to English radio programmes helps me get used to how fast native speakers talk.
children.cn: 贾欣：听英语广播节目帮助我习惯了母语者说话的速度。
children.notes: Listening to... 动名词短语作主语；get used to sth 习惯于……；how fast... 引导宾语从句。
children.statement: Listening to English radio programmes 在句中充当宾语。
children.explanation: 这是个动名词短语，在句中充当主语，谓语动词是单数形式 helps。
children.keywords: Listening to, radio programmes, get used to, fast
children.highlight: Listening to, helps me, get used to how

children.text: I also repeat what I hear to help myself to experience the feeling of the language.
children.cn: 我也模仿听到的内容，以帮助自己体验这门语言的语感。
children.notes: what I hear 宾语从句；to help myself 动词不定式作目的状语；experience the feeling of 体验……的感觉。
children.statement: what 引导的从句是宾语从句，充当 repeat 的宾语。
children.explanation: repeat 是及物动词，后面的 what I hear 充当其宾语，属于宾语从句。
children.keywords: repeat, hear, help myself, experience, feeling
children.highlight: repeat what I, to help myself to, the feeling of

children.text: Sometimes I even record my voice so I can listen to myself and compare my pronunciation with the radio host's!
children.cn: 有时我甚至录下自己的声音，这样我就可以听自己的录音，并将我的发音与广播主持人的发音进行对比！
children.notes: so 引导目的状语从句（口语中省略了that）；compare ... with ... 将……与……对比；host's 指代 host's pronunciation。
children.statement: compare my pronunciation with the radio host's 中的 host's 是名词所有格。
children.explanation: host's 后面省略了 pronunciation，所有格起到了指代作用，避免重复。
children.highlight: record my voice so, listen to myself, compare my, with the

children.text: My biggest headache is how to be polite in English.
children.cn: 我最头疼的是如何在英语中做到礼貌。
children.notes: biggest headache 最大令人头疼的事；how to be polite 疑问词+不定式结构在句中充当表语。
children.statement: how to be polite 在句中作定语修饰 headache。
children.explanation: how to be polite 在系动词 is 之后充当表语，说明最头疼的事情是什么。
children.highlight: My biggest headache is, how to be

children.text: It's so much easier to just say "Open the window!", but in English that can sound really terrible.
children.cn: 直接说“打开窗户！”要容易得多，但在英语中那听起来真的很糟糕（不礼貌）。
children.notes: It's easier to do sth 做某事要更容易，it是形式主语，后面的不定式是真正主语；sound 连系动词，后接形容词 terrible 作表语。
children.statement: sound 在这里是实义动词，意为“发出声音”。
children.explanation: sound 在这里是系动词，意为“听起来”，后面接形容词 terrible 作表语。
children.highlight: It's so much, to just say, but in, sound really

children.text: I have to think about who I'm talking to and then decide whether to say, "Open the window, please!" or "Could you open the window, please?" or even longer "Would you mind opening the window, please?"
children.cn: 我必须考虑我在和谁说话，然后决定是说“请打开窗户！”，还是说“你能打开窗户吗？”，或者更长一点“你介意打开窗户吗？”
children.notes: think about 考虑；who I'm talking to 宾语从句作 about 的宾语；whether to say ... or ... 决定是说……还是……；Would you mind doing sth? 你介意做某事吗？
children.statement: Would you mind doing 后面必须接动词的原形形式。
children.explanation: mind 后面必须接动名词形式（doing），例如 mind opening，不能接动词原形。
children.keywords: think about, talking to, decide, whether, Would you mind
children.highlight: have to think about, decide whether to, Would you mind opening

children.text: Li Rui: Request Types by Relationship & Vocabulary Headaches
children.cn: 李瑞：不同人际关系的请求表达与新词汇带来的头疼问题
children.notes: 李瑞指出语气取决于与对方的关系亲疏，并表达了对大量新词汇难以记忆和使用的烦恼。
children.statement: 李瑞认为与地位高或年长的人说话，请求时应该使用委婉语气，比如 'Would you mind...'。
children.explanation: 李瑞在帖子中说，如果和比自己地位高或年长的人说话，应该说 'Would you mind opening the window, please?'。

children.text: Li Rui: Yeah, that's really hard!
children.cn: 李瑞：是的，那真的很困难！
children.notes: really hard 确实很难。
children.statement: hard 是名词，表示“困难”。
children.explanation: hard 在这里是形容词，作系动词 is 的表语，表示“困难的”。
children.highlight: that's really

children.text: I think it all depends on who you're talking to.
children.cn: 我认为这完全取决于你在和谁说话。
children.notes: depend on 取决于，依靠；who you're talking to 宾语从句作 on 的宾语。
children.statement: depend on 之后接宾语从句，on 后面可以省略。
children.explanation: on 作为介词，引导宾语从句时是不能省略的，因为整个从句充当介词 on 的宾语。
children.highlight: I think it, depends on who

children.text: If I'm talking to a close friend, I can use short requests, like "Open the window"—our relationship is close and we're equals, so I only need a few words to bridge the gap between us.
children.cn: 如果我是在和好朋友说话，我可以使用简短的请求，比如“打开窗户”——我们关系很近，身份平等，所以我只需要几个词就能消除我们之间的隔阂。
children.notes: If 引导条件状语从句；equals 名词复数，平等的人；bridge the gap between us 桥接我们之间的鸿沟/消除隔阂。
children.statement: bridge the gap between us 意为“架起沟通的桥梁，缩小差距”。
children.explanation: 这是一个固定的比喻表达，意思是通过某些手段或语言来消除彼此的距离或隔阂。
children.keywords: close friend, requests, equals, bridge the gap
children.highlight: If I'm talking to, like, we're equals, to bridge the gap between

children.text: But if I'm talking to someone who isn't very close to me, I must make my request longer—and I must make it a question, not a demand, e.g., "Could you open the window, please?"
children.cn: 但如果我在和一个关系不太亲密的人说话，我必须把我的请求说得更长——而且我必须把它变成一个提问，而不是要求，例如，“你能打开窗户吗，请？”
children.notes: who 引导定语从句修饰 someone；make + 宾语 + 宾补 (make my request longer, make it a question)；demand 强硬要求，命令。
children.statement: who isn't very close to me 引导的是一个非限制性定语从句。
children.explanation: 这是一个限制性定语从句，用来限定修饰不定代词 someone，中间没有逗号隔开。
children.highlight: talking to someone who, make my request, make it a, not a

children.text: If I'm talking to someone senior to me, then I should say, "Would you mind opening the window, please?"
children.cn: 如果我在和一个比我年长或地位高的人说话，那我应该说，“你介意打开窗户吗，请？”
children.notes: senior to me 比我年长/地位高（注意是用 to，不用 than）；Would you mind doing...? 委婉语气的请求。
children.statement: senior to me 相当于 older than me 或 higher in rank than me。
children.explanation: senior / junior 具有比较意义，但后接表示比较对象的介词 to，而不是 than。
children.keywords: someone senior, should say, Would you mind
children.highlight: someone senior to me, then I should, Would you mind opening

children.text: For me, vocabulary is my biggest problem—there are just SO MANY new words!
children.cn: 对我来说，词汇是我最大的问题——有太多太多新词了！
children.notes: there are so many 有如此之多；vocabulary 词汇。
children.statement: so many 后面只能接可数名词复数形式。
children.explanation: many 修饰可数名词复数，而 much 则用来修饰不可数名词。
children.keywords: vocabulary, biggest problem, so many, words
children.highlight: For me, is my, there are just

children.text: I can't keep all the new vocabulary straight in my head, and I certainly can't remember how to use them all properly.
children.cn: 我脑子里没办法把所有的新词汇整理得清清楚楚，当然也记不住该如何正确地使用它们。
children.notes: keep sth straight 把某事理顺/搞清楚；remember how to do 记得该如何做；properly 妥善地，正确地。
children.statement: how to use them all properly 是“疑问词+不定式”结构，作 remember 的宾语。
children.explanation: 本结构由疑问词 how 加上动词不定式构成，整体在句中作及物动词 remember 的宾语。
children.highlight: can't keep all, straight in my, remember how to, properly

children.text: HELP!
children.cn: 救命！/救救我！
children.notes: 感叹句，呼救。
children.statement: HELP 在这里是名词，充当独立成分。
children.explanation: 这是动词原形构成的祈使句，在紧急或需要帮助的情况下使用，用于呼救。
children.highlight: HELP
